steps.title: Use sign and steepness
steps.content: The data trend upward, so the slope must be positive. That eliminates the other choices. The trend is fairly steep: as $x$ increases by about $5$, $y$ increases by about $9$, so the slope is close to $\frac{9}{5}=1.8$, not $0.2$.

steps.title: Distinguish C from D using the slope magnitude
steps.content: $y=0.2x+1.8$ would be almost flat. The matching choice, $y=1.80x+0.2$, has the steep positive slope shown by the scatterplot.

steps.title: Spot-check with a representative point
steps.content:
Using the point (5, 9) visible near the center of the plot:
The matching choice: $y = 1.80x + 0.2$ — within 0.2 of 9. ✓
$y = 0.2x + 1.8$ — far below the actual value of 9. ✗
D is correct.
